metadata.title: Core Korean: Daily Routines and Food
metadata.source_description: Supplemental A1 vocab selected from coverage gaps

item.notes: Coverage gap: family. High-utility family noun for introducing and talking about household members.
item.image_prompt: Polished illustration of Korean parents standing together in a bright modern living room, natural family portrait style, no text.

image.prompt: Polished illustration of Korean parents standing together in a bright modern living room, natural family portrait style, no text. Stylish, contemporary editorial illustration for an adult language learner in their 30s. Keep it warm, playful, and visually engaging without looking childish. Use clean composition, rich color, and a polished modern textbook feel. If the scene includes people, depict Korean people in a natural contemporary Korean setting. No text in the image.

cards.back_html:
parents
부모님
bumonim

Examples

  • 부모님은 서울에 사세요.
    My parents live in Seoul.
Coverage gap: family. High-utility family noun for introducing and talking about household members.
Source: New vocab proposal • coverage-gap • family

부모님
parents
bumonim

Examples

  • 부모님은 서울에 사세요.
    My parents live in Seoul.
Coverage gap: family. High-utility family noun for introducing and talking about household members.
Source: New vocab proposal • coverage-gap • family

image.prompt: Polished illustration of a Korean adult relaxing on a sofa in a cozy modern apartment living room, quiet weekend mood, no text. Stylish, contemporary editorial illustration for an adult language learner in their 30s. Keep it warm, playful, and visually engaging without looking childish. Use clean composition, rich color, and a polished modern textbook feel. If the scene includes people, depict Korean people in a natural contemporary Korean setting. No text in the image.
